stories.headline: Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic to Lead Claude Pre-Training Research Team
stories.key_takeaway: Andrej Karpathy joins Anthropic's pre-training team to build a new research group that will use Claude itself to accelerate the development of future Claude models.

pull_quote.text: I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R&D.

stories.article_excerpt: In a move that sent a jolt through the AI industry, Andrej Karpathy — OpenAI co-founder, former Tesla AI director — announced he is joining Anthropic.

stories.headline: Japan Megabanks Set to Access Anthropic Claude Mythos as Finance Minister Confirms Deal
stories.key_takeaway: Japan's Finance Minister confirmed MUFG, SMBC, and Mizuho will receive Claude Mythos access within two weeks, marking the first non-Western institutions admitted to the restricted rollout.

pull_quote.text: Cyberattacks against the financial industry could immediately trigger credit uneasiness. We must win the battle surrounding AI.

stories.article_excerpt: Japan's Finance Minister confirmed Friday that MUFG, SMBC, and Mizuho will receive access to Anthropic's Claude Mythos within two weeks.

stories.headline: Anthropic Opens Milan Office as EMEA Revenue Surges Ninefold Year Over Year
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic's EMEA run-rate revenue has grown 9x and large enterprise accounts 10x year over year, driving a six-city European footprint with Milan as the latest addition.

pull_quote.text: After France and Germany, Italy is a natural next step.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic is planting its flag in Italy this month, opening a Milan office that adds a sixth European city to its international footprint.

stories.headline: Cerebras Soars 68 Percent in Nasdaq Debut Pushing AI Chipmaker to $95 Billion Market Cap
stories.key_takeaway: Cerebras's 68% Nasdaq debut — the largest US tech IPO since Uber in 2019 — signals investor conviction that AI inference demand has outgrown Nvidia's monopoly.

pull_quote.text: If you ask Anthropic, if you ask OpenAI, they have vastly more demand for their offering than they have compute to make it.

stories.article_excerpt: Wall Street agreed on Cerebras. Shares of the AI chipmaker soared 68% on their first day of trading, closing at $331.07.

stories.headline: AI Search Startups Surge as Exa Labs Raises $250 Million at $2.2 Billion Valuation
stories.key_takeaway: Exa Labs and Parallel Web Systems have together raised over $350 million, reflecting investor conviction that the AI search infrastructure layer is the most strategic battleground.

pull_quote.text: Most other search providers actually wrap other search engines and therefore cannot compete on quality/latency/cost.

stories.article_excerpt: The race to rewire how the world finds information just got a fresh infusion of capital — and the numbers are staggering.

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Personal Finance With Plaid Integration Across 12,000 Banks
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI launched a Plaid-powered personal finance feature in ChatGPT for Pro subscribers, connecting 12,000+ financial institutions via GPT-5.5.

pull_quote.text: Finances are deeply personal and shaped by individual goals, priorities, and everyday realities.

data_points.label: ChatGPT users asking financial questions monthly
Former Mint users scattered after shutdown

stories.article_excerpt: On May 15, OpenAI launched a personal finance feature inside ChatGPT that lets Pro subscribers connect their bank accounts.

stories.headline: Kore.ai Ships Artemis Platform to Deploy Production AI Agents in Days Instead of Months
stories.key_takeaway: Kore.ai's Artemis platform uses a declarative Agent Blueprint Language to collapse enterprise multi-agent AI deployment from months to days.

pull_quote.text: Enterprise AI is entering its third wave, where governance, observability, and trust define success at scale.

stories.article_excerpt: Kore.ai launched its Artemis edition of the Agent Platform on May 21, promising enterprises a path from AI experimentation to production.

stories.headline: Camunda Unveils ProcessOS Agentic Operating System for Enterprise AI Orchestration
stories.key_takeaway: Camunda's ProcessOS layers four AI agents over the full business process lifecycle, delivering governance-by-design with AWS-native deployment.

pull_quote.text: Every process in your enterprise is legacy — it was designed for a world where AI did not exist.

stories.article_excerpt: In front of 1,200 enterprise leaders gathered in Amsterdam, Camunda CEO Jakob Freund unveiled ProcessOS — a new intelligence layer.

stories.headline: Cloudflare Builds High Performance Multi-GPU LLM Infrastructure With Custom Infire Engine
stories.key_takeaway: Cloudflare's Rust-written Infire inference engine runs frontier LLMs across multi-GPU configurations at the network edge, outperforming vLLM on throughput.

pull_quote.text: For most models, utilizing both pipeline parallelism and tensor parallelism in tandem provides the best balance of throughput and latency.
pull_quote.attribution: Michelle Chen, Kevin Flansburg, Vlad Krasnov

data_points.label: Faster than vLLM on H100 NVL

stories.article_excerpt: Cloudflare built Infire, a Rust-based inference engine that runs frontier LLMs across multiple GPUs more efficiently than Python alternatives.

stories.headline: Chinese AI Models Overtake US Rivals Capturing 61 Percent of Global API Token Traffic
stories.key_takeaway: Chinese AI models captured 61% of global OpenRouter token consumption by leveraging 10-20x cost advantages over US rivals.

pull_quote.text: Rather than competing directly on chip superiority, the competition now centers on token factory economics.

data_points.label: Peak Chinese model share on OpenRouter
Chinese tokens processed in one week

stories.article_excerpt: In the week ending February 24, 2026, something quietly historic happened: Chinese AI models overtook US rivals in global API token traffic.

stories.headline: Hadrian Releases Open Source OpenHack Tool for AI-Powered Vulnerability Discovery in Code
stories.key_takeaway: Hadrian open-sourced OpenHack under the MIT License, an AI workflow for source code vulnerability discovery that runs inside Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.

pull_quote.text: Attackers have workflows like this already. We'd rather hand defenders the same scaffolding we use internally.

data_points.label: Vulnerabilities found in Dutch govt software

stories.article_excerpt: When Hadrian's researchers pointed a commodity LLM at open-source applications used by Dutch government agencies, they found hundreds of vulnerabilities.

stories.headline: MOSS Framework Enables AI Agents to Self-Evolve Through Autonomous Source Code Rewriting
stories.key_takeaway: MOSS lets AI agents autonomously rewrite their own production source code to fix structural failures, more than doubling benchmark performance in a single cycle.

pull_quote.text: Since routing, hook ordering, state invariants, and dispatch live in code rather than in any text artifact, an entire class of structural failure is physically unreachable from the text layer.

stories.article_excerpt: An AI agent that can diagnose its own recurring failures, rewrite the source code responsible, and test the patches — all without human help.

stories.headline: Bipartisan Senate Group Introduces FARM AI Act to Expand Artificial Intelligence for American Farmers
stories.key_takeaway: A six-senator bipartisan coalition introduced the FARM AI Act targeting two gaps blocking AI adoption on farms — shortage of grant capital and trained rural workers.

pull_quote.text: The bill addresses two critical gaps facing farmers: a lack of capital to adopt AI tools and a shortage of trained workers in rural communities to implement them.
pull_quote.role: President, North Carolina Life Sciences Organization

stories.article_excerpt: A six-senator bipartisan coalition introduced legislation on May 21 that would embed AI research and workforce training into USDA programs.

stories.headline: Meta Tracked Employee Keystrokes and Screenshots to Build AI Training Data Before Mass Layoffs
stories.key_takeaway: Meta's mandatory Model Capability Initiative captured keystrokes, screenshots, and mouse movements with no opt-out — then laid off 8,000 employees days later.

pull_quote.text: No there is no opt out on your work provided laptop.

stories.article_excerpt: Before Meta cut roughly 8,000 employees on May 20, it had already been harvesting something else from them: their work patterns and keystrokes.

stories.headline: Japan Forms 36-Entity Public-Private Task Force to Counter AI Cybersecurity Threats
stories.key_takeaway: Japan convened a 36-entity working group chaired by Mizuho's CISO before its megabanks even received Claude Mythos access, treating AI vulnerability tools as a systemic national security risk.

pull_quote.text: Cyberattacks against the financial industry could immediately trigger credit uneasiness. We must win the battle surrounding AI.

stories.article_excerpt: Japan assembled a 36-member cross-sector working group to identify and neutralize systemic risks from AI vulnerability discovery tools.
